sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Levante UD has shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, with a knack for late goals but struggling defensively with no clean sheets in 4 games. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, is in strong form with 3 consecutive wins and an unbeaten streak of 6 matches, often scoring first and showing resilience in tight games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Celta Vigo holds a slight edge with 1 win and 1 draw, including a 2-0 victory at Levante’s home ground in 2021. Matches between these teams often see both sides scoring (7 of 9 games), but tend to have fewer than 2.5 goals in recent meetings.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Levante’s Etta Eyong has been a standout with 6 goals this season, overperforming his xG, while Carlos Espí is in form with a 7.7 average rating over the last 5 matches. For Celta Vigo, Iago Aspas remains a threat with 3 goals and an 8.0 rating in recent games, supported by Borja Iglesias’ clinical finishing.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Levante struggles with ball possession (40.4% average, 19th in the league) and defensive solidity, conceding 18 goals this season. Celta Vigo’s ability to create chances and score in crucial moments, especially late in games, could exploit Levante’s vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Levante sits 16th with 9 points, having scored 14 goals but with a poor defensive record (18 conceded, 16th in the league). Celta Vigo, 14th with 10 points, has a balanced attack and defense, conceding fewer goals (13) and showing consistency with frequent draws (7 this season).

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Celta Vigo’s current form, head-to-head dominance, and Levante’s defensive issues, the away side is slightly favored to win. Sofascore’s prediction of a 40% chance for an away win aligns with recent trends, though a tight contest with both teams scoring is likely.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Celta Vigo leads Levante UD 1-0 in a tightly contested match at the Ciudad de Valencia. The away side struck late in the first half through Óscar Mingueza, capitalizing on their dominance in possession and territorial control. Levante, despite creating a couple of significant opportunities, failed to convert and suffered a major setback with a red card, leaving them with ten men for the second half. This numerical disadvantage could heavily influence the remainder of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Levante UD's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD showed promise in attack early on, creating some big chances but failing to find the back of the net. Their inability to finish has kept them trailing, and the red card to Unai Vencedor just before the break has severely hampered their prospects. Defensively, they struggled to contain Celta Vigo's pressure in the final third, and with reduced numbers, maintaining solidity at the back will be a significant challenge in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Celta Vigo's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Celta Vigo controlled much of the game with superior ball possession and frequent entries into the final third. Their persistence paid off with a well-taken goal by Óscar Mingueza, who has been a standout performer. Despite not creating clear-cut chances early on, their ability to exploit spaces and maintain pressure led to the breakthrough. With Levante down to ten men, Celta will look to capitalize on their numerical advantage and extend their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Celta Vigo started strongly, dominating the early stages with consistent attacking spells. Levante responded with periods of sustained pressure in the middle of the half, showing intent to push forward. However, momentum shifted back to Celta Vigo towards the end of the half, culminating in their goal just before the break. These shifts suggest that Celta holds the upper hand going into the second half, especially with their ability to build attacks against a reduced opposition.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Levante UD, Etta Eyong could be crucial in the second half despite missing a big chance earlier. His presence up front might be their best hope for an equalizer if they can muster counter-attacks. On the other side, Óscar Mingueza has been instrumental for Celta Vigo, not only scoring but also contributing to their attacking build-up. Additionally, Borja Iglesias could exploit the extra space with Levante's reduced numbers and might be a threat in the box.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Celta Vigo pushing for another goal to secure the game, taking advantage of their extra man. Levante UD will need to focus on defensive organization while seeking rare opportunities on the counter. Given the dynamics of the first half and the current situation, Celta appears better positioned to control the tempo. Expect the away side to create more chances, while Levante’s task of holding firm and finding a way back into the game looks increasingly difficult.
